name: Colquitt County Teacher
description_short: Colquitt EMC is a not-for-profit electric membership cooperative in South Georgia serving 48,527 members across seven counties with reliable electricity delivery and community programs.
description_long:
Colquitt Electric Membership Corporation (Colquitt EMC) is a not-for-profit electric membership cooperative headquartered in South Georgia with 90 years of service history. Founded as a member-owned utility, it operates as a cooperative rather than a traditional for-profit utility company. The organization serves a seven-county region including Berrien, Brooks, Colquitt, Cook, Lowndes, Tift, and Worth counties. Colquitt EMC operates 73,787 connected meters across 9,547 miles of distribution lines, serving 48,527 member-households and businesses.

Colquitt EMC's primary service is the delivery of safe, reliable, and affordable electricity to residential and commercial members. Beyond utility service, the cooperative offers bill payment options, outage reporting through an Outage Center, and application services for new members seeking to connect to their grid. The organization maintains a digital platform for account management and customer support, accessible through their website at colquittemc.com.

As a not-for-profit cooperative, Colquitt EMC is distinguished by its member-owned structure and community reinvestment focus. Rather than maximizing shareholder profits, the cooperative returns excess revenues to members and invests in community development. The organization actively supports local education through a scholarship program for residents within member households attending colleges or technical colleges in their service territory, requiring a minimum 2.5 GPA. They also partner with Georgia 4-H and engage in public safety initiatives like Safe Digging Month awareness campaigns.

Colquitt EMC functions as a utility provider rather than a consumer finance company. While they offer essential electric service to their region, they do not provide financial products, loans, credit services, or banking functions beyond bill payment processing. Members seeking financial services would need to access those through separate banking or credit institutions. The cooperative's strength lies in reliable utility delivery and community commitment rather than consumer finance offerings.
services: Online bill payment and Quick Pay options
New service applications and connection requests
Educational scholarships for member household residents
Energy efficiency consultation and landscaping advice
pros: Not-for-profit cooperative structure means excess revenues are returned to members rather than paid to shareholders
90 years of established service history with 48,527 active members in the region
Operates extensive infrastructure with 9,547 miles of distribution lines ensuring broad coverage
Active scholarship program for member households pursuing higher education with minimum 2.5 GPA requirement
Community engagement through partnerships with Georgia 4-H and public safety initiatives
Online bill payment and outage reporting tools available through website
Serves both residential and commercial members in seven-county region
cons: Limited to service territory of seven South Georgia counties; not available nationwide
Provides only utility service; does not offer financial products, loans, or banking services
No apparent credit products or financial assistance programs despite being categorized as banking
best_for: Residential and commercial electricity customers in Berrien, Brooks, Colquitt, Cook, Lowndes, Tift, or Worth counties
Students within member households seeking educational scholarships
Community members interested in member-owned cooperative utility benefits
diagnosis: Colquitt EMC is a utility cooperative providing electricity service to South Georgia, not a consumer finance provider. The banking categorization appears to be a miscategorization error, as this company offers no financial products, loans, credit services, or banking functions—only electricity distribution and community programs for cooperative members.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
